title: Creamy Lemon Chicken Orzo Soup
description: This Greek-inspired creamy lemon chicken orzo soup is a hearty, flavorful comfort food that's packed with tender chicken, fresh vegetables, and aromatic herbs. The bright lemon flavor and creamy texture make it an irresistible healthy soup that the whole family will love.
ingredients: 1 lemon, juice and zest (about 3 Tablespoons juice)
instructions: Heat 1 1/2 Tablespoons olive oil and 1 1/2 Tablespoons butter in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Once butter is melted, add 3/4 cup diced onion, 1 cup diced carrots, and 1 cup diced celery. Sauté for 4-5 minutes, until softened. Add 3 cloves minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds, stirring constantly so garlic doesn't burn.

Add 1 1/2 lbs chicken breasts, 2 teaspoons chicken bouillon paste, 1/2 teaspoon Italian seasoning, 1/4 teaspoon dried thyme, 1/4 teaspoon dried rosemary, and 8 cups low-sodium chicken broth. Stir to dissolve the bouillon paste. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 15-18 minutes, until chicken reaches an internal temperature of 165°F at the thickest part.
Remove chicken to a cutting board. Cover loosely with aluminum foil and allow to rest for 5 minutes before chopping or shredding into bite-sized pieces.
Stir 1 cup dry orzo into the pot. Increase heat slightly to maintain a gentle simmer and cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking on the bottom, until orzo is tender.

Reduce heat to low. Stir in the juice and zest of 1 lemon, 1/3 cup slightly warmed heavy cream, and 1/4 cup fresh chopped parsley. Return the chopped chicken to the pot. Season generously with salt and pepper to taste. Taste and add additional bouillon paste or lemon juice if needed.
notes: Orzo will continue to absorb liquid as soup sits; the soup will thicken considerably. Add extra warm broth when reheating leftovers.
Warm the cream before adding to prevent any risk of curdling from the acidic lemon juice. To warm, microwave for 15-20 seconds or let it sit at room temperature while the soup cooks.

If storing leftovers, the orzo will absorb most of the broth overnight. Thin with additional chicken broth when reheating. Alternatively, cook orzo separately and add to individual bowls to keep leftovers broth-y.
For gluten-free: use gluten-free orzo pasta and ensure chicken bouillon paste is gluten-free.
